key_aspects: Comprehensive TypeScript configuration with strict type checking enabled
Path alias @/* pointing to ./src/* for cleaner imports
ES2022 target with NodeNext module resolution
Decorator support for routing-controllers and tsyringe libraries
Comprehensive include/exclude patterns for src, prisma, and scripts directories

config_sections.strict_type_checking: All strict flags enabled (strict: true with individual overrides)
config_sections.additional_checks: noUnusedLocals, noUnusedParameters, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noImplicitReturns, noFallthroughCasesInSwitch enabled
config_sections.emit: Output to ./dist with source maps and declaration files
config_sections.special_features: Experimental decorators and metadata emission enabled

patterns_discovered.description: Explicit enabling of all strict mode flags despite 'strict: true' already encompassing them, suggesting emphasis on clarity and explicit intent
patterns_discovered.significance: Indicates priority on type safety and adherence to project requirements (referenced as CRITICAL per CLAUDE.md)

patterns_discovered.description: Configuration organized into logical sections with comments (Language and Environment, Strict Type-Checking, Additional Checks, Emit, Interop Constraints, etc.)
patterns_discovered.significance: Demonstrates structured approach to configuration management and maintainability

patterns_discovered.description: Single path alias @/* mapped to ./src/* with baseUrl set to '.'
patterns_discovered.significance: Enables cleaner import statements throughout the codebase, reducing relative path complexity

patterns_discovered.description: Decorator support (experimentalDecorators, emitDecoratorMetadata) included for routing-controllers and tsyringe dependency injection
patterns_discovered.significance: Configuration anticipates framework needs beyond basic TypeScript setup

patterns_discovered.description: ts-node configuration included with ESM support and experimental specifier resolution
patterns_discovered.significance: Enables direct TypeScript execution during development and testing

gotchas_discovered.description: Both 'strict: true' and individual strict flags are specified, which is technically redundant since 'strict: true' enables all of them
gotchas_discovered.impact: No functional impact, but adds verbosity; could be confusing for maintenance
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Either rely on 'strict: true' alone or document why each flag is explicitly listed

gotchas_discovered.description: While other interop constraints are strictly set, 'verbatimModuleSyntax: false' allows import erasure that may conflict with strict checking philosophy
gotchas_discovered.impact: May mask certain type-safety issues with import statements
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider setting to true if strict module semantics are required by project standards

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Test Files Excluded from TypeScript Compilation
gotchas_discovered.description: All .spec.ts, .test.ts files and __tests__ directories are excluded from compilation
gotchas_discovered.impact: Tests won't be type-checked by tsconfig; requires separate test runner configuration
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Ensure test runners (Jest, Vitest) have their own TypeScript configuration or type checking

gotchas_discovered.description: While improving build speed, skipLibCheck bypasses type checking of declaration files
gotchas_discovered.impact: Potential type inconsistencies from third-party libraries may go undetected
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Monitor for type issues from dependencies; consider periodic full checks without skipLibCheck

key_accomplishments: Created production-ready tsconfig.json with comprehensive strict type checking
Implemented path alias @/* for cleaner imports as per project requirements
Configured support for experimental decorators needed by routing-controllers and tsyringe
Organized configuration with clear section comments for maintainability
Included appropriate include/exclude patterns for multi-directory TypeScript project
approach_outcome.compliance: Configuration aligns with CLAUDE.md requirements (Rules 3.3 strict mode and 3.4 path aliases referenced in comments)

recommendations.recommendation: Consider removing individual strict flags if keeping 'strict: true', or add explanatory comments if each flag serves a specific purpose beyond the base strict setting
recommendations.rationale: Improves readability and reduces maintenance burden

recommendations.recommendation: Evaluate setting 'verbatimModuleSyntax: true' to enforce stricter module semantics if project requires it
recommendations.rationale: Would align with the overall strict type-checking philosophy

recommendations.recommendation: Create a separate tsconfig.test.json that extends the base config but includes test files, for use by test runners
recommendations.rationale: Enables type checking of test files while keeping them excluded from production builds

recommendations.recommendation: Add a README or configuration guide explaining the strict settings and path alias usage for team onboarding
recommendations.rationale: Helps new developers understand project's type-safety requirements

recommendations.recommendation: Periodically review skipLibCheck impact; consider periodic full validation builds in CI/CD pipeline
recommendations.rationale: Ensures dependency type issues don't accumulate over time
